criteria.name: Code solves challenges presented in aims/objectives

descriptors.text: Code does not address the stated aims or objectives
Code partially addresses some aims with significant gaps
Code addresses most aims and objectives effectively
Code fully addresses all aims and objectives with clear solutions
No evidence of iterative design process
Some evidence of iteration but not systematic
Clear evidence of systematic iterative design with improvements
Excellent iterative design with well-documented changes driven by evaluation

criteria.name: Approach is fit for purpose without drastic oversights

descriptors.text: Approach has fundamental flaws or major oversights
Approach is mostly sound but has notable gaps
Approach is well-suited to the problem with minor limitations
Approach is excellently matched to the problem with no significant oversights
Code is poorly formatted, hard to read, inconsistent style
Code is clean, well-formatted, readable with consistent style and naming
Files and code are disorganised with no clear structure
Sensible file structure with logical separation of concerns

criteria.name: Evidence of collaboration and team work

descriptors.text: No evidence of collaboration or external resource use
Some engagement with external resources
Good engagement with external resources, peer review, and discussion
Excellent evidence of collaboration, peer review, and wide resource utilisation

criteria.name: Evidence of milestones and reflective updates

descriptors.text: No milestones or reflective updates evident
Clear milestones with reflective updates such as Gantt charts and resource allocation
Very basic technical challenge with minimal complexity
Moderate technical challenge
Significant technical challenge requiring advanced skills
Highly ambitious technical challenge pushing boundaries
No novel aspects; entirely conventional approach
Some novel elements in the approach
Notable novelty in approach or application domain
Highly novel and original technical contribution

criteria.name: All parts of the system working

descriptors.text: Major parts of the system are broken or non-functional
All parts of the system are working as intended
No UI evaluation or assessment conducted
UI evaluation conducted with clear findings
Tests are not documented or are poorly described
Tests are well documented with clear descriptions
No error handling or crashes on invalid input
Effective error handling with graceful degradation
No systematic testing approach
Systematic testing regime with appropriate test cases designed
Testing methods not justified
Testing methods clearly justified with rationale
No formative user evaluation conducted
Formative evaluation with representative stakeholders providing actionable feedback
No summative user evaluation conducted
Summative evaluation conducted measuring outcomes against goals
